quote: Two-partner secretion (TPS) systems use beta-barrel proteins of the Omp85-TpsB superfamily to transport large exoproteins across the outer membranes of Gram-negative bacteria. The Bordetella FHA/FhaC proteins are prototypical of TPS systems in which the exoprotein contains a large C-terminal prodomain that is removed during translocation.
note: FhaB (filamentous hemagglutinin, FHA) is the TpsA exoprotein; cognate TpsB transporter is FhaC (locus_tag BP1884, UniProt P35077). BP1879 is the old_locus_tag in the Tohama I (BX470248) annotation; RefSeq protein WP_010930610.1.

quote: The ShlA hemolysin of Serratia marcescens is secreted across the outer membrane by the ShlB protein; ShlB belongs to the two-partner secretion system (type Vb), a subfamily of the Omp85 outer membrane protein assembly and secretion superfamily.
note: TpsB partner is ShlB (locus_tag SMDB11_RS18525 / old SMDB11_3736, ShlB/FhaC/HecB family hemolysin secretion protein); shlBA operon, the two genes are adjacent. SMDB11_RS18530 (old SMDB11_3737) is RefSeq-annotated generically as 'filamentous hemagglutinin N-terminal domain-containing protein' (the PF05860 TpsA/Haemagg_act signature), placed here as the genome-located ShlA-family TpsA in the lab reference strain Db11. The historical ShlA characterization (UniProt P15320) is from an earlier non-genome strain; primary_ref quote is the type-Vb framing for the Serratia ShlA/ShlB pair.

quote: FhaC proved to be unable to mediate secretion and activation of HpmA, the cognate secretory partner of HpmB. In contrast, ShlB, the accessory protein of the closely related Serratia marcescens hemolysin, was able to activate and secrete HpmA.
note: HpmA = calcium-independent hemolysin, the TpsA exoprotein. TpsB partner is HpmB (locus_tag PMI2056, UniProt B4F0Z8); hpmBA operon. PMI2057 is old_locus_tag; current RefSeq locus PMI_RS10140.

quote: HxuA is secreted by its specific transporter HxuB. HxuA/HxuB belongs to the so-called two-partner secretion systems (TPSs) that are characterized by a conserved N-terminal domain in the secreted protein which is essential for secretion.
note: HxuA = heme-haemopexin binding protein, the TpsA exoprotein. TpsB partner is HxuB (hxuCBA locus; HxuB UniProt P45355). locus_tag HI_0264 in Rd KW20.

quote: Wild-type H. ducreyi strain 35000HP contains two genes, lspA1 and lspA2, whose encoded protein products are predicted to be 456 and 543 kDa, respectively ... These results indicate that the LspA1 and LspA2 proteins of H. ducreyi are involved, directly or indirectly, in the antiphagocytic activity of this pathogen.
note: LspA1 = Large supernatant protein 1, a filamentous-hemagglutinin-family (PF05860) TpsA exoprotein. TpsB partner is LspB (locus_tag HD_1155, UniProt G1UBA6). locus_tag HD_1505 confirmed (UniProt Q7VLE8). Quote is from the foundational lspA1/lspA2 paper; the TPS/TpsB framing for the Lsp system is established in Guerin 2017 (10.3389/fcimb.2017.00148).

quote: Wild-type H. ducreyi strain 35000HP contains two genes, lspA1 and lspA2, whose encoded protein products are predicted to be 456 and 543 kDa, respectively ... These results indicate that the LspA1 and LspA2 proteins of H. ducreyi are involved, directly or indirectly, in the antiphagocytic activity of this pathogen.
note: LspA2 = Large supernatant protein 2, a second TpsA exoprotein in H. ducreyi 35000HP sharing the LspB TpsB transporter. locus_tag HD_1156 confirmed via GenPept (protein gi 33148493, /gene=lspA2, AE017143.1:923760..938519); LspB is the immediately adjacent HD_1155. lspA1 (HD_1505) is the paralogous TpsA elsewhere on the chromosome.

quote: Bacterial contact-dependent growth inhibition (CDI) is mediated by the CdiB/CdiA family of two-partner secretion proteins. CDI systems deploy a variety of distinct toxins, which are contained within the polymorphic C-terminal region (CdiA-CT) of CdiA proteins.
note: CdiA = contact-dependent growth-inhibition toxin, the TpsA exoprotein. TpsB partner is CdiB (cdiBAI operon, cdiB upstream of cdiA). locus_tag ECP_4580 in UPEC 536 (UniProt Q0T963).

quote: Upon cell contact, the toxic C-terminus of the TpsA family member CdiA, called the CdiA-CT, inhibits the growth of CDI(-) bacteria ... A requirement for bcpO in function of BcpA (the TpsA family member) was demonstrated, indicating that bcpAIOB define a novel class of TPS system.
note: BcpA = Burkholderia CdiA-class contact-dependent inhibition toxin, a TpsA-family exoprotein; bcpAIOB locus. TpsB partner is BcpB (the TpsB family member, downstream in bcpAIOB). locus_tag BTH_I2723 (old) = BTH_RS26080 (current RefSeq), chromosome I NC_007651.1.

quote: The two-partner secretion pathway in Gram-negative bacteria consists of a TpsA exoprotein and a cognate TpsB outer membrane translocator protein ... we characterized the functional domains of the Haemophilus influenzae HMW1B protein, a TpsB protein that interacts with the H. influenzae HMW1 adhesin.
note: HMW1A (HMW1) = high-molecular-weight adhesin, the TpsA exoprotein. TpsB partner is HMW1B (locus_tag R2846_0713, adjacent; hmw1ABC operon). locus_tag R2846_0714 in strain R2846 (= strain 12, the strain HMW1 was characterized in), chromosome NC_017452.1.

quote: The two-partner secretion pathway in Gram-negative bacteria consists of a TpsA exoprotein and a cognate TpsB outer membrane translocator protein ... we characterized the functional domains of the Haemophilus influenzae HMW1B protein, a TpsB protein that interacts with the H. influenzae HMW1 adhesin.
note: HMW2A (HMW2) = second high-molecular-weight adhesin, a TpsA exoprotein paralogous to HMW1A. TpsB partner is HMW2B (hmw2ABC operon). locus_tag R2846_1072 in strain R2846, chromosome NC_017452.1. Quote characterizes the HMW1/HMW1B TPS system; HMW2A is the well-established paralog secreted by its own HMW2B.

quote: We have characterized one of these components, encoded by the PA4625 gene, as a putative adhesin and designated it cdrA. CdrA shares structural similarities to extracellular adhesins that belong to two-partner secretion systems. The cdrA gene is in a two gene operon that also encodes a putative outer membrane transporter, CdrB.
note: CdrA = c-di-GMP-regulated biofilm-matrix adhesin, the TpsA exoprotein (secreted as a 220 kDa proprotein, processed to 150 kDa). TpsB partner is CdrB (cdrAB operon, cdrB = PA4624). locus_tag PA4625 in PAO1, chromosome NC_002516.2.
